Grid Correction Suggestions v2

Grid: 303x303 Clues matched to grid: 18,586/28,922 (64.3%) Numbered cells in grid: 24,733
Suggested: flip to BLACK: 9,182 (3,014 high-conf + 6,168 low-conf) Suggested: flip to WHITE: 4,038 (1,563 high-conf + 2,475 low-conf)
White cell (no suggestion)
Black cell (no suggestion)
Suggests BLACK (high) — both Across+Down clues exist but grid only supports one direction
Suggests BLACK (low) — one clue direction doesn't match; could be grid error or clue label error
Suggests WHITE (high) — both Across+Down clues exist but grid only supports one direction
Suggests WHITE (low) — one clue direction doesn't match; could be grid error or clue label error
How to read this: Each highlighted cell is a place where the clue database and grid CSV disagree. Solid colors (high confidence) mean both an Across and Down clue exist for a numbered cell but the grid only supports one — the grid is very likely wrong there. Faded colors (low confidence) mean a single clue's direction doesn't match the grid — could be the grid OR a mislabeled clue direction. Hover any cell for details. Scroll to zoom, drag to pan.
Dan's method: Upper-left quadrant (rows 0–151, cols 0–151) done by hand + formulas, then mirrored via 180° rotation. Any error in the UL quadrant will appear in all four quadrants. Use "Highlight UL quadrant" to see the boundary. Center = row 151, col 151.
Scroll to zoom | Drag to pan | Click cell for details
